en.extract:
Josephine's lorikeet (Charmosyna josefinae) is a species of parrot in the family Psittaculidae.
It is found in the highlands of New Guinea (central range and Cyclops Mountains).
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.


Description
Josephine's lorikeet is red with green wings, a yellow-tipped tail, dusky blue, orange bill, yellow eyes, and black flanks.


Diet
It feeds on nectar, pollen, flower buds, and sometimes soft fruits.


References

'''Josephine's lorikeet''' ('''''Charmosyna josefinae''''') is a species of [[parrot]] in the family [[Psittaculidae]].
It is found in the highlands of [[New Guinea]] (central range and [[Cyclops Mountains]]).
Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].

== Description ==
Josephine's lorikeet is red with green wings, a yellow-tipped tail, dusky blue, orange bill, yellow eyes, and black flanks.

== Diet ==
It feeds on nectar, pollen, flower buds, and sometimes soft fruits.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parrots.org/encyclopedia/category/parrotlets/|title=Parrotlets - WPT Parrot Encyclopedia|first=World Parrot|last=Trust|website=www.parrots.org}}</ref>
